title: Iridescent Shell Ion Extraction Lab
description: A chemistry lab where the user operates a PrimaryIonDrive beam apparatus to selectively extract rare-earth ions from layers of an IridescenceAbalone shell specimen. The user must calibrate beam energy, frequency, and focus across multiple shell layers while obeying conservation of charge and mass, with full undo/redo support for each extraction step.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) implementing an interactive 3D chemistry lab. Load IridescenceAbalone.glb as a specimen mounted on a rotating platform at scene center, and PrimaryIonDrive.glb as an ion beam apparatus positioned above-right, aimed at the shell. Use a PerspectiveCamera at (0,3,5) with OrbitControls. Add hemisphere light (sky 0xccddff, ground 0x443322, intensity 1.2) plus a directional light casting shadows and a point light near the ion drive nozzle tinted cyan. The shell has 5 virtual layers each containing a different ion type (Ca²⁺, Sr²⁺, Mg²⁺, Na⁺, CO₃²⁻). Display an HTML overlay panel with: beam energy slider (1-10 eV), frequency selector (UV/Visible/IR), focus dial (narrow/medium/wide), a target layer dropdown (1-5), an Extract button, and Undo/Redo buttons. Each extraction step: user sets all 4 parameters then clicks Extract. Animate a glowing beam (cyan RayHelper or custom ShaderMaterial cylinder) from the ion drive to the shell. Each ion type requires a specific energy+frequency combo; wrong combos yield partial extraction with waste. Track a conservation ledger: total charge and mass extracted must balance with ions removed from each layer. Show the ledger in a side panel updating in real-time. After extraction, tint the corresponding shell region (use vertex colors or overlay mesh) to show depletion. Require 10-12 steps to fully extract all 5 ion types across layers. If conservation is violated (e.g., extracting only cations without balancing anions), flash a red warning and block further extraction until the user corrects via undo. Implement full undo/redo stack storing each extraction action's parameters and results. Add subtle shell iridescence animation by slowly rotating environment map sampling. On completion (all ions extracted in balance), play a success particle burst from the shell and display a summary of extraction efficiency.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  beamEnergy: number,         // current beam energy in eV (1-10)
  beamFrequency: string,         // selected frequency band (UV, Visible, IR)
  beamFocus: string,         // beam focus setting (narrow, medium, wide)
  targetLayer: number,         // currently selected shell layer (1-5)
  extractionCount: number,         // total number of extraction operations performed
  totalChargeExtracted: number,         // cumulative ionic charge extracted (in elementary charges)
  totalMassExtracted: number,         // cumulative mass extracted in atomic mass units
  currentLayerIonCount: number,         // remaining ion count in the currently targeted layer
  wasteGenerated: number,         // cumulative waste mass from incorrect parameter combinations
  undoStackSize: number,         // number of operations available to undo
  isExtracting: boolean,         // true when beam animation is active
}
